rulings.Case Title: NATIONAL CREDIT ACCEPTANCE, INC., A CALIFORNIA VS. DANIEL YAUYEN WONG et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, September 8, 2008, line 6. DEFENDANT NATIONAL GENERAL CONTRACTING INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant shifted the burden of producing evidence by establishing that Wells Fargo's relationship was with Wong and National General Contracting Co. (a sole proprietorship), not defendant. Plaintiff has failed to produce evidence of a triable issue of fact. Plaintiff's procedural arguments are not persuasive. Plaintiff had the opportunity to respond to the motion. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: NATIONAL CREDIT ACCEPTANCE, INC., A CALIFORNIA VS. DANIEL YAUYEN WONG et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, September 8, 2008, line 6. DEFENDANT WALTER CHAN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Chan shifts the burden of producing evidence. Plaintiff's evidence, the checks signed by Chan, creates a triable issue of fact as to causes of action for quasi contract, open book account and account stated. Motion for summary adjudication is granted on alter ego. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: NATIONAL CREDIT ACCEPTANCE, INC., A CALIFORNIA VS. DANIEL YAUYEN WONG et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, September 8, 2008, line 6. DEFENDANT WALTER CHAN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Chan shifts the burden of producing evidence. rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, September 8, 2008, line 8. PLAINTIFF EMPORIUM MALL, LLC'S Demurrer To Defendants' Answer/Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend. No opposition filed. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: INTERSTATE FIRE AND CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY VS. CLEVAND WRECKING COMPANY
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, September 8, 2008, line 9. DEFENDANT CLEVAND WRECKING COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. Plaintiff in its opposition asserts that defendant engaged in tortious conduct that gave rise to the loss. That is not plead. On its face, the complaint fails to state a claim for equitable subrogation. The decision in Patent Scaffolding Co. v. Wm. Simpson Constr. Co. (1967) 256 Cal App 2d 506 and Bramalea Calif., Inc. v. Reliable Interiors, Inc. (2004) 119 Cal App 4th 468 are authority to sustain the demurrer for failure to state a claim. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: THE JOHN STEWART COMPANY VS. LORRAINE BENDER et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, September 8, 2008, line 12. PLAINTIFF THE JOHN STEWART COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. A hearing is required. Plaintiff has standing; the police report is admissible; defendant's request for a continuance fails to specify discoverable facts that would defeat the motion; it is not clear from the record whether plaintiff has considered the letter of 6/27/08 from the property manager. =(302/PJM)
